notes: All numbers under `verified_product_output` are direct UI output from the live HuggingFace Space deployment, captured via Playwright on 2026-05-10.
The motivating `57%` headline is research-cited (Johari et al., KDD 2017, 'Peeking at A/B Tests'), not a product measurement. It is the reason the tool exists; it is not what the tool itself emits.
Trust-layer counts (tests, coverage gate) come from `D:\AB_TEST` repository state as of HEAD 7a627459.

motivation.what_it_means: False-declaration rate of classical fixed-horizon A/B testing under continuous peeking, in simulation.
motivation.source: Johari, Pekelis, Walsh, Koomen — 'Peeking at A/B Tests', KDD 2017. Cited via Optimizely Stats Engine whitepaper.
motivation.label: literature · motivation, not product output

srm_check.verdict: SRM detected — check randomization or tracking

public_safe_claim: AB Test Research Designer turns a hypothesis into a launch-ready plan: live wizard emits sample size 75,514 / variant and 21 days for the canonical binary config; group-sequential O'Brien-Fleming boundaries (4.05 / 2.86 / 2.34 / 2.02) make interim looks safe; SRM detector catches a 49.0% / 51.0% split as χ² = 20, p ≈ 8·10⁻⁶; CUPED reduces continuous-metric sample size by 25% (591 → 443); Bayesian credible interval lands at 277 per variant. The 57% headline is the textbook reason the tool exists — Johari et al. KDD 2017 — not a self-measurement.
